### Post by clinux on 2008-09-04
Hello,

I know this is a common problem, but after a lot of searching i still haven't found a solution. Opera crashes when i'm watching youtube videos, especially when i'm seeking or pausing. I tried reinstalling flash but i had little success. The weird thing is that flash works on other sites and i have no problem with firefox.

here is my pluginpath.ini:
```
Opera Preferences version 2.1
; Do not edit this file while Opera is running
; This file is stored in UTF-8 encoding

[Paths]
/home/tp/.mozilla/plugins=1
/usr/lib/opera/plugins=1
/usr/lib/flashplugin-nonfree=1
/usr/lib/mozilla/plugins=1
```

thanks

---

### Post by Liviu-Theodor on 2008-09-04
If flash+youtube runs under Firefox, but does not under Opera, then the problem relies with the Opera browser. I think you should direct your question to a Opera forum or to the Opera developers.

---

### Post by gandaran on 2008-09-04
opera is very stable if you running the final opera 9.51 or 9.52 versions, some other opera snapshots do have crash problems, if you have the final release version, I suggest to delete the hidden home .opera folder (backup the opera folder first), start-up opera again and check with youtube videos if it still crashes.

---

### Post by clinux on 2008-09-04
I'm running 9.52 and deleting /home/$(USER)/.opera didn't help.
